title: Service with Named Ports
description:
Create a multi-port Service exposing HTTP (port 80) and a metrics endpoint (port 9090) from a single Pod, referencing container ports by name instead of number.

**Why this matters**: Named ports decouple Service definitions from exact port numbers. If a container port changes, only the Pod spec needs updating — the Service manifest stays the same. This is also required by Prometheus ServiceMonitors.

**Common Pitfalls**: Named port values in containerPorts must exactly match the targetPort strings in the Service. A name typo silently creates a port with no backing target.
steps: Define two named containerPorts on the Pod: name=http port=80, name=metrics port=9090
In the Service, add two port entries referencing targetPort by name (http and metrics)
Verify both ports appear in kubectl get svc app-server-svc
Verify endpoints exist for the Service
hints: containerPort entries accept a 'name' field — this name is what you reference in targetPort
targetPort can be a string (port name) or an integer (port number)
Each Service ports entry needs its own 'name' field when there are multiple ports
Use 'kubectl describe svc app-server-svc' to see both port mappings

editor_config.initial_code:
# --- App Pod with two named ports ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: app-server
  labels:
    app: app-server
spec:
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
    - name: # TODO: name the HTTP port (e.g. 'http')
      containerPort: 80
    - name: # TODO: name the metrics port (e.g. 'metrics')
      containerPort: 9090
---
# --- Multi-port Service using named targetPorts ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: app-server-svc
spec:
  selector:
    app: app-server
  ports:
  - name: http
    port: 80
    targetPort: # TODO: reference the named port (must match containerPort name above)
  - name: metrics
    port: 9090
    targetPort: # TODO: reference the named metrics port

_solution.code_solution:
# --- App Pod with two named ports ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: app-server
  labels:
    app: app-server
spec:
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
    - name: http
      containerPort: 80
    - name: metrics
      containerPort: 9090
---
# --- Multi-port Service using named targetPorts ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: app-server-svc
spec:
  selector:
    app: app-server
  ports:
  - name: http
    port: 80
    targetPort: http
  - name: metrics
    port: 9090
    targetPort: metrics
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

echo "Waiting for app-server Pod to be running..."
timeout=60
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  phase=$(kubectl get pod app-server -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}' 2>/dev/null || echo "Pending")
  if [ "$phase" = "Running" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 3
  ((elapsed+=3))
done
if [ "$phase" != "Running" ]; then
  echo "✗ app-server Pod did not reach Running state"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ app-server Pod is running"

PORT_COUNT=$(kubectl get svc app-server-svc -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(len(json.loads(sys.stdin.read())))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [ "$PORT_COUNT" -lt 2 ]; then
  echo "✗ Service app-server-svc has $PORT_COUNT port(s), expected 2"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Service app-server-svc has $PORT_COUNT ports"

HTTP_TARGET=$(kubectl get svc app-server-svc -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports[?(@.name=="http")].targetPort}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$HTTP_TARGET" != "http" ]; then
  echo "✗ HTTP port targetPort is '$HTTP_TARGET', expected named port 'http'"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ HTTP port uses named targetPort 'http'"

METRICS_TARGET=$(kubectl get svc app-server-svc -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports[?(@.name=="metrics")].targetPort}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$METRICS_TARGET" != "metrics" ]; then
  echo "✗ Metrics port targetPort is '$METRICS_TARGET', expected named port 'metrics'"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Metrics port uses named targetPort 'metrics'"

ENDPOINTS=$(kubectl get endpoints app-server-svc -o jsonpath='{.subsets[0].addresses}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ -z "$ENDPOINTS" ] || [ "$ENDPOINTS" = "null" ]; then
  echo "✗ Service app-server-svc has no endpoints"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Service app-server-svc has endpoints"

POD_HTTP_PORT=$(kubectl get pod app-server -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].ports[?(@.name=="http")].containerPort}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$POD_HTTP_PORT" != "80" ]; then
  echo "✗ Pod http named port is '$POD_HTTP_PORT', expected 80"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Pod containerPort 'http' is 80"

POD_METRICS_PORT=$(kubectl get pod app-server -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].ports[?(@.name=="metrics")].containerPort}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$POD_METRICS_PORT" != "9090" ]; then
  echo "✗ Pod metrics named port is '$POD_METRICS_PORT', expected 9090"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Pod containerPort 'metrics' is 9090"

echo "✓ Named ports Service validation passed"
exit 0
